provenance.pin_detail: no version specified (resolves to whatever the registry serves now)

privilege_notes: runs fetched package code at launch

findings.title: Installed on-the-fly via a package runner
findings.detail: Server 'tradememory-protocol' is fetched and executed at launch via 'uvx'; package spec: tradememory-protocol.
findings.recommendation: Install the server as a pinned dependency and invoke the local binary, or vendor it. If a runner is unavoidable, pin an exact version (see unpinned_source).

findings.title: Package source is unpinned or mutable
findings.detail: Server 'tradememory-protocol' package spec 'tradememory-protocol' is unpinned (pin strength: none): no version specified (resolves to whatever the registry serves now).
findings.recommendation: Pin an exact version (pkg@1.2.3) or a git commit SHA. Avoid @latest and branch refs.

evidence.reason: no version specified (resolves to whatever the registry serves now)

tools.name: store_trade_memory
tools.description:
Store a trade decision with full context into memory.

Call this after executing a trade to build your memory bank.
Include market_context and reflection for better recall later.

data_categories.label: Financial / payment data

evidence.snippet:
a trade to build your memory bank.
Include market_context and r

data_categories.rationale: Direct financial exposure and a regulated data class.

tools.name: recall_similar_trades
tools.description:
Find past trades with similar market context.

Use this before making a trade to learn from past experience.
Returns trades with their reflections and outcomes.
Uses OWM scoring when episodic memories exist, falls back to keyword matching.

tools.name: get_strategy_performance
tools.description:
Get aggregate performance stats per strategy.

Use this to evaluate which strategies are working and which need adjustment.

data_categories.label: Public / aggregate data

evidence.snippet: Get aggregate performance stats per strateg

data_categories.rationale: Already public or non-identifying — minimal exposure.

tools.name: get_trade_reflection
tools.description:
Get the full context and reflection for a specific trade.

Use this to deep-dive into a particular trade's reasoning and lessons.

tools.name: remember_trade
tools.description:
Store a trade into OWM multi-layer memory with automatic updates.

Writes to episodic memory and automatically updates semantic (Bayesian),
procedural (running averages), and affective (EWMA confidence/streaks).
Also writes to trade_records for backward compatibility.

tools.name: recall_memories
tools.description:
Recall memories using OWM outcome-weighted scoring.

Queries episodic and semantic memories, scores them by outcome quality,
context similarity, recency, confidence, and affective modulation.
Returns ranked memories with score breakdown.

tools.name: get_behavioral_analysis
tools.description:
Get behavioral analysis from procedural memory.

Returns aggregate trading behavior stats: hold times, disposition ratio,
lot sizing variance, and Kelly criterion comparison.

data_categories.label: Public / aggregate data

evidence.snippet:
m procedural memory.

Returns aggregate trading behavior stats: hold

data_categories.rationale: Already public or non-identifying — minimal exposure.

tools.name: get_agent_state
tools.description:
Get the current agent affective state (confidence, risk, drawdown).

Returns confidence level, risk appetite, drawdown percentage,
win/loss streaks, equity tracking, and a recommended action
based on current drawdown severity.

tools.name: create_trading_plan
tools.description:
Create a prospective trading plan that activates when conditions are met.

Stores a rule-based plan in prospective memory. The plan stays active
until triggered, expired, or manually cancelled.

tools.name: check_active_plans
tools.description:
Check active trading plans against current market context.

Queries all active prospective plans, expires any past their expiry date,
and matches remaining plans against the provided context.

tools.name: evolution_fetch_market_data
tools.description:
Fetch OHLCV market data from Binance for evolution analysis.

Downloads historical price bars for backtesting and pattern discovery.
Use this before discover_patterns or run_backtest to get data.

candidate_capabilities.title: Outbound network / data egress
candidate_capabilities.rationale: A tool that makes outbound calls can exfiltrate anything it can read (including the server's own credentials) to an attacker-chosen host.

tools.name: evolution_discover_patterns
tools.description:
Discover trading patterns from market data using LLM analysis.

Uses Claude to analyze OHLCV data and generate candidate trading patterns
with entry/exit conditions. Each pattern can be backtested afterward.

data_categories.label: Public / aggregate data
data_categories.rationale: Already public or non-identifying — minimal exposure.

tools.name: evolution_run_backtest
tools.description:
Backtest a candidate pattern against historical OHLCV data.

Takes a pattern dict (from discover_patterns) and runs a vectorized
backtest. Returns fitness metrics: Sharpe ratio, win rate, trade count,
max drawdown, total PnL.

data_categories.label: Public / aggregate data

evidence.snippet:
Sharpe ratio, win rate, trade count,
max drawdown, total PnL. Can

data_categories.rationale: Already public or non-identifying — minimal exposure.

tools.name: evolution_evolve_strategy
tools.description:
Run full evolution loop — generate, backtest, select, eliminate.

Multi-generation strategy evolution: generates candidate patterns via LLM,
backtests on in-sample data, validates survivors on out-of-sample data,
eliminates weak hypotheses. Returns graduated strategies and graveyard.

tools.name: evolution_get_log
tools.description:
Get the log of past evolution runs from this session.

Returns a list of all evolution runs with their results, including
graduated strategies, graveyard, token usage, and backtest counts.
Data is in-memory (resets on server restart).

candidate_capabilities.title: Reads secrets / credentials / env

evidence.snippet:
duated strategies, graveyard, token usage, and backtest counts.
D

candidate_capabilities.rationale: A tool that surfaces secrets pairs dangerously with any network_egress tool on the same server — read-then-send is a complete exfil chain.
candidate_capabilities.validation_reason: 'Token usage' in context refers to LLM API token consumption metrics, not credential or secrets access.

tools.name: export_audit_trail
tools.description:
Export Trading Decision Records for audit and compliance review.

Provides a complete, tamper-evident record of trading decisions including
the memory context (similar trades, beliefs) that informed each decision.

tools.name: verify_audit_hash
tools.description:
Verify the integrity of a Trading Decision Record.

Recomputes the SHA256 data_hash from stored inputs and compares with
the hash computed at decision time. A mismatch indicates tampering.

data_profile.note: Rating reflects high-confidence matches; financial matched only in prose (medium confidence) and would raise the rating if confirmed in review.

toxic_combinations.title: Read-then-send exfiltration chain
toxic_combinations.detail: The server can both access secrets/credentials and make outbound network calls — a path to exfiltrate them to an attacker-chosen host.
toxic_combinations.validation_reason: Exfil risk requires both secrets_access and network_egress; secrets_access claim is false positive, eliminating the attack path.

validated_out.reason: 'Token usage' in context refers to LLM API token consumption metrics, not credential or secrets access.
Exfil risk requires both secrets_access and network_egress; secrets_access claim is false positive, eliminating the attack path.
